title: [bug] request_maintenance: tighten scenario 72 instruction-substring step-defs to sentence-scoped containment (lead-pn9 §4.4 closure)
description:
§4.4 closure on lead-pn9. The Reviewer signed off 5/6 scenarios (71, 73, 74, 75, 76) but flagged scenario 72 (d87ccb133fa64d2f — 'canonical primer activation instruction names prereqs and failure mode') as having loose step-defs in tests/conftest.py:

- then_body_directs_path_verification (L5451-5479): whole-body 3-token conjunction (verify-verb OR 'command -v') AND ('path' substring) AND ('before' substring + 'monitor' substring), with no proximity/sentence binding.
- then_body_directs_refuse_with_diagnostic (L5487-5530): same whole-body conjunction shape (refuse_verb + diagnostic_marker + missing_marker), no sentence binding.

Reviewer demonstrated false-positive: bare-stripped primer body 'Before you read further: if inotifywait and stdbuf are missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a diagnostic.' still passes because (a) 'sufficiency check' elsewhere supplies has_verify_verb via 'check', (b) 'PATH' satisfies has_path_token via the host-prereq bullet ('packages being present on PATH'), (c) 'before' anywhere + 'monitor' anywhere satisfies has_monitor_temporal.

Fix shape per Reviewer: mirror scenario 71's exclusion-clause step-def at L5398-5439 (sentence-aware regex scan over sentence-broken body, requires the activation-verb + hook-pattern coupling within a single sentence). Tighten both L5451-5479 and L5487-5530 to require their full token-conjunction within a single sentence.

Gherkin text is FINE. Canonical primer is FINE. Only the step-defs are too permissive.

Vehicle: request_maintenance — flat test-infrastructure tightening; no new scenarios; existing scenario 72 pinned behavior unchanged.

title: brief-003 realization revision: replace SessionStart-hook activation with CLAUDE.md-primer instruction (router arms Monitor at session start)
description: Test-harness BC diagnosed a hook-model mismatch in brief 003's canonical templates: Claude Code awaits SessionStart hooks synchronously, so a foreground 'inotifywait -m' invocation never returns and startup hangs / times out. Schema + host prereqs are fine; the mismatch is structural — synchronous hook delivery is incompatible with a long-running watcher. Brief 003's two invariants (every shop reactive on session start; activation honors brief 001 encapsulation) still hold. The realization changes: in place of a SessionStart hook running inotifywait, the canonical CLAUDE.md primer carries an explicit instruction telling the router (Claude session) to arm the Monitor tool on a 'stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to <watch-dir>/' pipeline at session start. Monitor IS the in-session primitive that delivers the 'background process whose stdout streams as notifications' semantic the hook was faking. This bead carries the revision: PO authors superseding scenarios (numbered 71+, tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates) that retire the hook-model-bound scenarios (57/58/59/65/68 wholesale; 70 keeps as schema-conformance with shrunk scope) and pin the new realization (CLAUDE.md primer per shop type contains activation-instruction substring; instruction names host prereqs + failure mode; bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md contains the instruction). title: request_bugfix: brief 003 implementation (12 scenarios 57-68, post-supersession re-dispatch)
description:
Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 scenarios (57-68) to shopsystem-templates, carried as request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation (BC already has bootstrap capability; brief 003 extends it).

BACKGROUND
==========
The lead-xkh chain hit a coordination gap. Sequence:

- lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios 57-68 for brief 003: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json, bootstrap extension) → BC clarified on contradiction with prior-pinned scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin in bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature).
- lead-5mz (request_bugfix, carried only scenario 69 — the supersession removing scenario 51's settings.json clause, leaving AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) → BC work_done complete, scenario 1a6e90189f9c2ade attested, supersession landed clean.

But: lead-5mz carried ONLY scenario 69. Brief 003's 12 positive pins (.claude/settings.json writing, prereqs naming, venv-sync, etc.) were never delivered to the BC. lead-xkh still has a clarify in its outbox, so the BC's router will not re-process it. To get brief 003 implemented, the lead must send a NEW dispatch carrying the 12 brief 003 scenarios.

This is a protocol gap shape: the §4.4 closure assumption that lead-5mz's terminal work_done closes lead-xkh's chain doesn't hold because lead-5mz only carried the supersession scenario, not the brief 003 positive pins. Prior chains (lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e) worked because each follow-up bundled the prior scenarios. The lead-5mz dispatch instruction was wrong on this — it should have bundled.

LINKAGE
=======
- Predecessors: lead-xkh (assign_scenarios for brief 003, clarify-stalled in BC outbox), lead-5mz (closed; clean supersession of scenario 51).
- lead-xkh remains in_progress with a pointer to this new bead. lead-xkh closes implicitly when this new work_id's terminal work_done arrives in the BC outbox.

SUPERSESSION STATE
==================
- Prior scenario 51 (hash 7d64bb8ed5a3f656, compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin) is retired. Its settings.json clause was removed by lead-5mz.
- New scenario 69 (hash 1a6e90189f9c2ade, AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) lives in BC features/bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature.
- Brief 003's scenario 60 (bootstrap writes canonical .claude/settings.json per shop type) no longer conflicts with anything in the BC — the contradiction lead-xkh tripped on is resolved.

PAYLOAD (12 scenarios 57-68)
============================
Implements brief 003 items A–E:

- A (canonical .claude/settings.json templates ship from shop-templates): scenarios 57, 68.
- B (SessionStart inotifywait hook, four reference-realization properties): scenarios 58, 65.
- C (lead-shop settings.json composes with existing bd prime hook): scenario 59.
- D (bootstrap/update writes and re-pours .claude/settings.json; CLAUDE.md names activation host prereqs): scenarios 60, 61, 62, 63, 64.
- E (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv; bootstrapped shop resolves BC CLI invocations to live source on session start): scenarios 66, 67.

Scenario files (lead canonical authored locations):
  features/templates/57-canonical-claude-settings-json-templates-ship-from-shop-templates.gherkin
  features/templates/58-canonical-claude-settings-json-declares-sessionstart-hook.gherkin
  features/templates/59-canonical-lead-settings-json-composes-with-existing-bd-prime-hook.gherkin
  features/templates/60-bootstrap-writes-canonical-claude-settings-json-per-shop-type.gherkin
  features/templates/61-update-re-pours-claude-settings-json-as-managed-file.gherkin
  features/templates/62-update-is-idempotent-on-already-current-claude-settings-json.gherkin
  features/templates/63-bootstrap-generated-claude-md-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/64-canonical-claude-md-primer-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/65-canonical-sessionstart-fails-loudly-on-missing-inotifywait.gherkin
  features/templates/66-bootstrapped-shop-resolves-bc-cli-invocations-to-live-source-on-session-start.gherkin
  features/templates/67-lead-bootstrap-installs-sibling-bc-clones-editable-into-product-venv.gherkin
  features/templates/68-canonical-bc-settings-json-does-not-name-lead-watch-target.gherkin

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (previously prepared, surfaced here since assign_scenarios was retired in favour of request_bugfix and request_bugfix's description IS the carrier for procedural framing)
=========================================================================================================================================================================================

- Scope item E realization framing: scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under repos/ on session start). Scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv). The two compose: 67 is one valid realization of 66, but 66 is the durable contract the Reviewer attests against, and 67 names the specific mechanism the canonical templates encode today. If a future realization replaces editable installs with a different live-source resolution (e.g., a path-aware shim), 66 remains intact and 67 gets superseded.

- Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait): the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait is missing on PATH; silently degrading to a no-watcher state is the failure mode this scenario pins against. The canonical settings.json should encode this property via the hook's startup command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary), not via a separate runtime probe.

- Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition): the lead-shop's canonical settings.json composes both the inotifywait activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart. Concretely the SessionStart entry has multiple hook commands, not a single replaced command. This is so the activation hook does NOT clobber bd prime (operational hygiene).

PROCEDURAL REMINDERS
====================
- Hash discipline via wire-form per lead-wgv: the shop-msg send CLI computes hashes from the wire-form scenario body. Pass --scenario-file flags only; do not pre-hash.
- venv activated per lead-zmi: source .venv/bin/activate before invoking shop-msg.
- Vehicle is request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation in the lead-xkh clarify: BC already carries bootstrap pins; brief 003 tightens/extends the existing bootstrap behavior with additional managed files and CLAUDE.md content. This is NOT new capability — bootstrap exists and writes managed files today; this dispatch adds .claude/settings.json to the managed set and extends the CLAUDE.md content the bootstrap writes.

EMPIRICAL PRE-STATE VERIFICATION
================================
- Verified BC's bootstrap capability already exists by inspecting BC features/bootstrap_cli_surface.feature, bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature, and bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature: scenarios for bootstrap writing managed agent files, writing CLAUDE.md, writing .gitignore, and invoking bd init are all pinned and green per lead-5mz's regression pin list. So Q1 (does the BC have the capability?) answer is YES — bootstrap exists, writes managed files; brief 003 extends the managed set.
- Verified BC does NOT pin .claude/settings.json today: grep '.claude/settings.json' across BC features/ produces no hits after lead-5mz landed. So the specific extension brief 003 names is unpinned — Q2 (does any scenario currently pin it?) is NO. Tightening, not flat change.
- Therefore: request_bugfix is the correct vehicle.

title: scenarios 57-68: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)
description:
Brief 003 (lead-0h1) — Event-driven shop activation. 12 scenarios extending the
shop-templates bootstrap surface to ship a canonical `.claude/settings.json`
per shop type plus host-prereq naming, and to install sibling BC clones
editable from the lead bootstrap.

The brief carries two invariants and five scope items:

- Invariant 1: every shop is reactive on session start (BC watches own
  `inbox/`; lead watches `repos/*/outbox/`).
- Invariant 2: activation honors brief 001's encapsulation — the
  watcher fires a wake signal; reading content still goes through
  `shop-msg`.
- Scope A: canonical `.claude/settings.json` per shop type (scenarios 57, 68).
- Scope B: SessionStart hook content with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait,
  alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged 2>&1 for startup-chatter
  visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming (scenarios 58, 59, 65).
- Scope C: bootstrap writes settings.json; update re-pours it as a
  bootstrap-managed file (scenarios 60, 61, 62).
- Scope D: prereq naming (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`,
  `stdbuf`) in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` and the canonical primer
  (scenarios 63, 64).
- Scope E: live-source resolution outcome + lead bootstrap installs
  sibling BC clones editable into the product venv (scenarios 66, 67).

## Empirically validated context

This session validated the stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipeline end-to-end
on lead-79q reconciliation: sub-second event-fire on `work_done`
arrival via the reference pipeline named in scope item B. The pattern
works. Scope items A/B/C/D codify the canonical shape so every
bootstrapped shop gets it by default.

## Pre-state cited

Pre-state verification on shopsystem-templates current state:

- Package data at `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files
  (`bc-implementer.md`, `bc-reviewer.md`, `lead-po.md`, `lead-architect.md`)
  + 2 CLAUDE.md primers under `templates/claude/` (`bc.md`, `lead.md`) +
  `gitignore.template`. No `.claude/settings.json` template exists.
- `src/shop_templates/cli.py`: bootstrap writes `.claude/agents/*.md`,
  top-level `CLAUDE.md` (from primer + `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution),
  top-level `.gitignore`, and invokes `bd init --skip-agents`. No
  `.claude/settings.json` is written. `update` re-pours managed
  agent files only — no settings.json handling.
- Empirical bootstrap of a probe directory
  (`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe
  --target /tmp/probe-003`) produced exactly: `CLAUDE.md`,
  `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md`, `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`.
  No `.claude/settings.json`. Exit 0.
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` scan: no existing scenarios
  pin SessionStart hooks, settings.json content, Monitor harness,
  inotifywait, or venv install mode.

All 12 scenarios target net-new capability — `assign_scenarios` is the
correct vehicle.

## Implementation notes (informational; bodies are the contract)

- Scope item E is realization-pinned by the PO via two scenarios:
  scenario 66 pins the outcome (live-source resolution on session
  start) and scenario 67 pins the install-shape (lead bootstrap
  installs sibling clones editable into the product venv). Scenario 67
  alone is the PO's leaning shape; the Implementer is free to add a
  hook-time refresh on top if pre-state on E surfaces drift cases not
  covered by 67 alone, or to treat 67 as sufficient.

- Scenario 65 pins the "loud fail on missing `inotifywait`" property.
  The Implementer must select a hook shape such that a host with no
  `inotifywait` on PATH surfaces a visible diagnostic — not a silent
  no-watcher state. A polling fallback that silently activates

notes:
2026-05-12 (PO subagent, brief 001 items B+D, round 3 follow-up) — BC clarify on lead-e4g (request_bugfix carrying scenario 46) confirmed the regression vulnerability is present in three sister subsections that scenario 46 does not pin: lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' (line 158), bc-implementer '## Your job' (line 26), bc-reviewer '## What you read' (line 15). The Implementer's textual FIX extended across all four templates correctly, but coverage only pinned one. Without parallel scenarios, future edits to the other three subsections could regress to bare-verb 'Read the inbox YAML' (or similar) and only the template-wide encapsulation scenarios (6adb97ba0d0498c5, ff97230bf1e36a81, f279d66b8313e3fc) would gate them — and the BC's clarify text correctly notes those are too weak for per-step discipline (a bare 'Read' step coexists with 'shop-msg read inbox' elsewhere in the same template, satisfying template-wide substring presence while leaving the procedural step ambiguous).

Scope decision (router): option (a) accepted — three parallel scenarios mirroring 46's shape. Not (b) (BC's textual fix needs scenario coverage to be load-bearing). Not (c) (tightening the template-wide encapsulation scenarios with per-step granularity would lose the presence/absence checks they currently make; per-subsection scenarios are the cleaner shape). Outline-form consolidation deferred — scenario 46 is already dispatched + hashed; a brief revision can fold all four into a Scenario Outline if the pattern continues to recur.

Authored: features/templates/47-lead-po-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/48-bc-implementer-your-job-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/49-bc-reviewer-what-you-read-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin. Each mirrors scenario 46 exactly: locate the subsection by heading boundary (## or ### until next heading), three Then conditions — (i) every step whose action is reading the mailbox names the literal CLI substring on the same step, (ii) no bare-verb regression mode, (iii) no path-shape regression mode. Vary only by template name, subsection heading text, and target substring (read outbox vs. read inbox).

Pre-state verification of BC's sketch: subsection headings as sketched (verified by grep). Two asymmetries noted but kept out of scope of this dispatch:
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 1 (line 28) names BOTH 'shop-msg read inbox' (reading) AND 'shop-msg pending inbox' (listing). The new scenario 48 pins only the read-inbox action, parallel to scenario 46's narrow target. The pending-inbox-listing action is already covered template-wide by scenarios 17/18 (uses-shop-msg-cli-for-mailbox-access). Same shape applies to bc-reviewer 'What you read' step 1 (lines 17-20) — scenario 49 pins only read-inbox.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' is H2 (## Your job); lead-po's target is H3 (### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI); bc-reviewer's target is H2 (## What you read); lead-architect's target (scenario 46) is H3. Scenario 46's locator clause 'ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###)' handles both depths uniformly; new scenarios reuse the same clause verbatim. Consistent.

Round-4 outlook (flag): With round 3 closing the four-template per-subsection-CLI-naming regression mode, the remaining brief-001 B+D regression-mode surface is:
- Procedural CLI naming for the OTHER mailbox actions inside the same/adjacent procedural subsections. Specifically: writing the outbox response. lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify'; lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4 cites the same. But no scenario currently pins per-step CLI naming for the RESPOND action. Same regression mode: a future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only the template-wide scenarios would gate it. If round 4 is desired, the same Scenario-Outline-amenable shape applies across all four templates' write-outbox / write-inbox actions.
- Procedural CLI naming for 'shop-msg respond work_done' (Implementer/Reviewer) and 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' (Implementer). bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 names 'shop-msg respond work_done'; 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section names 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation'; bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' names both 'shop-msg respond work_done' and 'shop-msg respond clarify'. None of these are pinned by per-step scenarios.

Recommendation: defer round 4 to a deliberate brief revision rather than chasing it dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline — and the cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly. Until then, the four parallel scenarios are correct and load-bearing.

Did NOT dispatch. Did NOT compose shop-msg send. Architect opens a request_bugfix dispatch bead and sends the three new scenarios as additive to lead-e4g's coverage. (Open question for Architect: dispatch as a separate work_id, or amend lead-e4g? The BC's clarify is open against lead-e4g; the cleanest closure is one new request_bugfix work_id whose description references lead-e4g's clarify as the trigger.)

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — docker-compose postgres, DSN via service name
description: Ephemeral /tmp cluster silently breaks shop-msg on container restart or system reboot. A compose.yml with a named postgres service gives a stable, named network endpoint reachable by service name inside Docker networks — the correct Docker Compose networking pattern. The default DSN must use the compose service name as the hostname (e.g. postgresql://postgres:5432/shopsystem if the service is named postgres), not localhost or 127.0.0.1 (which only works with network_mode: host). A devcontainer postStartCommand running docker compose up -d ensures the service is available on session start. Supersedes lead-cyc.
close_reason: superseded — compose needs host-bind volumes and attachable network

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — docker-compose postgres for shop-msg
description: Ephemeral /tmp cluster silently breaks shop-msg on container restart or system reboot. The default DSN points to a cluster at /tmp/pgdata (started with pg_ctl on port 5433, unix socket at /tmp/pgrun) — that path is not preserved across sessions. A well-known network location with a named Docker volume is needed for reliability across sessions and CI.
close_reason: superseded — DSN must use compose service name, not localhost

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging postgres backend
description: Current file/YAML storage walks directories per-BC; as the multi-BC topology grows this approach becomes fragile and loses durability. Replacing the storage backend with Postgres simplifies pending-query logic (single JOIN vs directory walking), adds transactional durability, and enables LISTEN/NOTIFY for push-style agent wakeup.

title: Brief 003 canonical settings.json templates use wrong Claude Code hook schema (bare type+command vs matcher+hooks wrapper)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 when user attempted 'claude' session start in shopsystem-test-harness after brief 003's bootstrap update.

Claude Code rejects the canonical settings.json with validation error:
  /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-test-harness/.claude/settings.json
   └ hooks → SessionStart → 0 → hooks: Expected array, but received undefined

Cause: brief 003's canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json wrote SessionStart entries in this shape:
  { 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }
But Claude Code's actual schema requires the matcher+hooks wrapper:
  { 'matcher': '', 'hooks': [{ 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }] }

The scenarios 57-68 pinned content substrings (SessionStart, stdbuf -oL inotifywait, watch-target tokens) but NOT the structural schema Claude Code validates against. The BC's Reviewer signed off because all 12 scenarios passed against the static-content checks; nothing exercised the file against real Claude Code.

Two facets:
(a) Canonical template content correction — fix the bc.json and lead.json package data to use matcher+hooks wrappers. shop-templates update propagates to existing shops.
(b) Scenario tightening — add a new scenario pinning the actual Claude Code hook schema (e.g., 'every SessionStart entry has a matcher key and a hooks array'). Without this, future regressions of the canonical templates won't be caught.

Resolution shape: dispatch request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates carrying both the canonical fix and the scenario tightening.

Local workaround applied today: hand-patched /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-test-harness/.claude/settings.json with the correct schema (commit 6865ff0 in the BC). Other already-bootstrapped shops will inherit the bug until canonical templates are fixed AND 'shop-templates update' is run.

Sister concern: brief 003's empirical validation focused on stdbuf-vs-buffering correctness (the lead-79q reconciliation pattern) but not on whether Claude Code itself would accept the file. title: Clarify protocol gap — clarify-resolution requires explicit re-dispatch carrying original scenarios
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-xkh→lead-5mz→lead-1pi chain. Sister concern to lead-rv9 (work_done blocked).

Pattern observed: BC clarifies on a dispatch (lead-xkh). Lead resolves the clarify by dispatching a follow-up (lead-5mz) carrying ONLY the resolution scenario (scenario 69). BC processes lead-5mz cleanly but does NOT re-process lead-xkh — because lead-xkh has a clarify in outbox, it's no longer 'pending' from BC's view. The original scenarios in lead-xkh remain unimplemented until the lead sends ANOTHER dispatch (lead-1pi) carrying them.

This is a coordination gap:
- The §4.4 closure narrative says 'terminal work_done implicitly closes the chain.'
- But that only works when the follow-up CARRIES the prior scenarios (as lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e did via accumulation).
- If the follow-up carries ONLY the resolution (e.g., a small supersession), the original scenarios are stranded.

Two design questions:
1. Should the BC's protocol re-process an inbox message after a clarify is responded to? (e.g., 'clarify in outbox does not finalize pending state; lead's next dispatch with matching work_id signals re-process')
2. Or should the lead-side protocol explicitly carry-forward prior unimplemented scenarios in every follow-up? (current de facto pattern, but easy to miss)
3. Or should we have a new message type 'clarify_resolved' (or similar) that signals 'process the originally-dispatched scenarios now'?

The current workaround (lead-1pi re-dispatches the 12 brief 003 scenarios + supersession context in description) works but produces wire-form hash drift (because the feature-title wrapping changes between dispatches) and isn't machine-readable for tracking which dispatch carries which scenarios.

Sister concerns:
- lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap)
- lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption)
- lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry)
- lead-yug (@supersedes tag convention)

All four-plus are accumulating in a 'catalog protocol gaps' cluster that would benefit from a coordinated brief revision once the test-harness or other priority work creates pressure to consolidate.

Concrete impact on lead-1pi: wire-form hashes for scenarios 57-68 differ from lead-xkh dispatch (e.g., 57 was d4b8aac19b34ea8c then; 1621b59b0ea8b20b now). Implementer's BDD attestation will use the new hashes. The brief 003 intent semantically equivalent.

title: Product venv installs drift from BC source after BC work lands (no auto-reinstall)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-79q dispatch.

The product venv at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/ has shop_msg installed as a regular package (not editable). When BCs update their source (lead-231.1 added 'pending' subcommand; lead-bwu/14e/ijr updated CLI behavior), the venv install does NOT update automatically. Lead-shop CLI invocations against shop-msg (and likely shop-templates similarly) see the OLDER installed surface, not the BC's live source.

Concrete impact today: shop-msg pending didn't exist in the venv install despite being present in BC source. Architect for lead-79q hit this and worked around by direct ls of inbox/outbox — a violation of brief 001 invariant 1 (encapsulation; CLI is sole messaging surface) forced by tooling staleness. Re-installed manually via 'pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging' which fixed it. shop-templates likely has the same issue (Architect for lead-79q also noted product venv shop-templates lacks bootstrap/update).

Two facets:
(a) Install mode: BCs should be installed editable (-e) so source changes flow without reinstall. Currently appears to be non-editable.
(b) Reinstall trigger: even if editable, fresh BC clones / version changes need a way to refresh. SessionStart hook in brief 003 could include 'pip install -e repos/*/' to keep the venv synced.

Adjacent / related:
- lead-zmi (shop-msg PATH ergonomics) — same theme, different facet.
- Brief 003 SessionStart hook design — should include venv-resync as part of activating each shop.

The 'ls workaround' the Architect used is a discipline concern: the lead-architect template's encapsulation guidance should be tightened to say 'if shop-msg pending fails, FIX THE TOOLING (reinstall/PATH/version) not bypass'. Worth a separate template-tightening bead if recurring.

title: bc-implementer template should require hash-recompute verification after @scenario_hash tag edits (drain of messaging-d64)
description:
Drain of messaging-d64 mechanism_observation from shopsystem-messaging, observed during lead-5cv.

Finding: bc-implementer canonical template does not require the Implementer to verify a @scenario_hash:<value> tag by piping the file through 'scenarios hash' before claiming the tag is correct. In lead-231.2 the Implementer wrote 4 fabricated hash values; Reviewer caught it but only via lead-side reconciliation cost (full §4.4 round-trip via lead-5cv).

Target BC: shopsystem-templates (the canonical bc-implementer template is the templates BC's product; messaging BC cannot self-fix).

Vehicle hint: request_bugfix (template-language tightening of unpinned existing behavior).

Natural fold-in with lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook for brief 001 — per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions). Both items tighten the bc-implementer canonical template via additional procedural discipline; both could be carried by one brief revision that consolidates 46-49 + write-mailbox cases + hash-verify step into a coordinated dispatch. Worth considering together when the next brief revision is authored.

Also adjacent: lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw vs wire form). Tightening hash-verify discipline in the template is load-bearing; ensuring the BC's bc-implementer knows WHICH form to verify against requires lead-wgv to be resolved first (or the discipline step to be explicit about it).

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook), lead-wgv (canonicalization), lead-5cv (original incident).

title: scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during lead-e4g dispatch (request_bugfix closing lead-bwu §4.4 loop).

The Architect observed: 'scenarios hash < features/templates/46-*.gherkin' (against the raw scenario body) produces 06328160e83d2995, while the hash 'shop-msg send' persists on the wire (validated by 'shop-msg read') is e6bdf2f33bfae0d1. The CLI computes against a wrapped+sentinel-tagged form, not the raw body.

This is the Slice-16-shaped mismatch named in the architect's role contract anti-rationalization section. Same shape almost surfaced in lead-231.2 / lead-5cv (Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values; Reviewer caught via 'cat file | scenarios hash' which DID match the post-edit canonical) — but that case's reconciliation worked because both Implementer and Reviewer used raw-body hashing on already-tagged feature files, where the @scenario_hash tag had been hand-edited. The two-canonicalization-domains problem didn't bite because both parties stayed within one domain.

For lead-e4g and any future dispatch, the canonical question matters:
- The BC's ScenarioPayload.hash == compute_scenario_hash(gherkin) enforcement uses which canonicalization?
- The 'scenarios hash' CLI computes which form?
- Implementers verifying tags need to match against what the catalog enforces.

Three resolution shapes:
(a) One canonical form across both. 'scenarios hash' wraps its input the same way shop-msg does; tag values across feature files / inboxes / outboxes are byte-identical for identical scenario semantic content.
(b) Two named forms with clear scope. 'scenarios hash --raw' and 'scenarios hash --wire' (or similar); documented when each applies.
(c) Status quo + documentation. Names the two domains and where each is canonical; templates and procedural reminders cite the right form.

(a) is the cleanest invariant; (b) is most explicit; (c) is lowest-touch but most failure-prone.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap), lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption) — same shape of latent protocol/canonicalization constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Adjacent: lead-5cv's Implementer-defect-and-Reviewer-catch worked because both used raw-body hashing on already-tagged files (within one canonicalization domain). A future dispatch where one party uses the wire-form hash and the other uses raw-body would silently disagree.

Concrete impact for current work: lead-e4g's @scenario_hash tag in the BC inbox is the wire form. The BC's Implementer / Reviewer must validate against the wire form, not via raw-body 'scenarios hash'. Worth a procedural reminder in the dispatch description (already included by Architect for lead-e4g).

title: work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).

title: Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept
description:
Surfaced by Architect during brief-001 items A+C dispatch on 2026-05-12.

During dispatch of lead-231.1 (AssignScenarios) and lead-231.2 (RequestBugfix) to shopsystem-messaging, the Architect used dotted child bead IDs (lead-231.1, lead-231.2) as work_ids. Both schemas accepted them — neither AssignScenarios.work_id nor RequestBugfix.work_id carries a pattern constraint (just str). But Clarify.work_id has pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which rejects dots.

This is a latent failure: if the BC sends Clarify on either lead-231.1 or lead-231.2, the BC-side 'shop-msg respond clarify --work-id lead-231.1' will fail schema validation. The protocol's symmetry across message types breaks at clarify-time.

What to do: align work_id patterns across catalog schemas. Three shapes worth considering — (a) tighten AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix/RequestMaintenance/WorkDone/MechanismObservation to share Clarify's pattern, constraining work_id naming uniformly; (b) loosen Clarify to match the others (just str), accepting more chaos but unblocking; (c) pick a shared canonical pattern (probably (a)'s shape with an explicit grammar) and apply uniformly with a single Pydantic type alias. Architect's call after pre-state verification.

Adjacent finding (do not scope here, just note): shop-msg has no recall command for in-flight dispatches, and the encapsulation invariant from lead-231 prohibits rm-ing inbox files. The two existing lead-231.1/.2 dispatches will tolerate this gap because they're more likely to round-trip via work_done than clarify, but recall-side encapsulation is a structural concern worth its own future brief.

Related: lead-231 (brief that surfaced this).

title: shop-templates bootstrap fails on existing .beads/ — needs idempotent or skip-bd-init mode
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 setting up shopsystem-test-harness as a canonical shop.

shop-templates bootstrap shells out to 'bd init --skip-agents'. When the target dir already has an initialized .beads/, bd init errors with 'Found existing Dolt database... Aborting.' Bootstrap exits 1 with no scaffold written.

This blocks bootstrap on existing shops with a beads workspace — exactly the 'wrap existing repo' case brief 002 was scoped to handle.

Workaround today: 'shop-templates update' (doesn't shell to bd init) creates .claude/agents/ + canonical settings.json; CLAUDE.md re-rendered manually via python invocation of _render_claude_md() since update is init-only on CLAUDE.md per brief 002 item D.

Resolution shapes:
(a) Bootstrap detects existing .beads/ and skips bd init (idempotent). Matches brief 002's 'wrap existing repo' invariant.
(b) Bootstrap gains --skip-bd-init flag for explicit user control.
(c) Update gains optional CLAUDE.md re-render flag for the case of already-bd-init'd shops migrating to canonical.

(a) + (c) together cover the wrap-existing case cleanly. Worth a brief revision in the bootstrap area.

title: Framework convention: @supersedes:<hash> tag for scenario replacement in inter-shop messaging
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-5mz dispatch (brief 003 §4.4 closure for lead-xkh).

When a new scenario supersedes a previously-dispatched one, the relationship is currently expressed only in prose (Gherkin Feature description block, dispatch description). The Architect dispatching lead-5mz noted that adding an @supersedes:<hash> tag at dispatch time would make the relationship machine-readable in the BC's scenario register, rather than buried in prose.

Concrete shape: catalog schema's @bc:<name> and @scenario_hash:<hash> are existing tag conventions injected by shop-msg send. @supersedes:<hash> would be a parallel injection at dispatch time (could be a --supersedes flag on shop-msg send, or auto-detected from scenario body, or set via PO's authoring tag).

Benefits:
- Machine-readable retire-from-feature-file instruction for the BC's Implementer.
- Audit trail in the BC's outbox when work_done attests against new scenarios.
- Potentially enables a future 'scenario register' surface (lead-otu) to track supersede chains.

Tradeoffs:
- Introduces a new tag convention; requires shop-msg schema or send-side tooling change.
- The prose mechanism works today; this is an ergonomic / discipline improvement.

Worth designing alongside the round-4 consolidation brief (lead-6t3) — that brief will already touch scenario discipline broadly. Could fold.

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook consolidation), lead-otu (scenario register surface), lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization).

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: PO authored scenarios 55 and 56 (parallel scenarios, not Scenario Outline). Scenario 55: bootstrap rejects missing --target with argparse-style error; mirrors scenario 54 (--shop-name) shape — exit 2, stderr names --target, no traceback / no TypeError, witness target dir untouched (no .claude/agents, no .beads, no CLAUDE.md, no .gitignore). Scenario 56: update rejects missing --target; same exit-2 + named-arg + no-traceback discipline, but the negation pins 'pre-existing shop scaffold is unchanged' (byte+mtime snapshot of .claude/agents/, plus CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/ unchanged), reflecting that update operates on an existing scaffold rather than creating one. Rationale for parallel-over-outline: (1) scenario 54 is flat, parallel preserves symmetry; (2) the negations meaningfully diverge — bootstrap asserts nothing-created against an empty dir, update asserts nothing-modified against an existing scaffold; (3) per-row readability matters for future debugging. title: bc-implementer singleton-inbox presumption constrains lead-shop dispatch cadence
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during brief-002 Architect pre-state verification.

The bc-implementer canonical template at repos/shopsystem-templates/.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md line 49 says verbatim 'There is exactly one file there.' The Implementer subagent's role contract presumes a singleton inbox. The bc-router CLAUDE.md does not enforce serialization either — its dispatch rule is 'any unprocessed inbox dispatches the Implementer' with no ordering guarantee.

Concrete consequence: the lead shop cannot dispatch a new message to a BC while another work_id is still in flight (still in the BC's inbox, not yet processed to outbox). Architect for brief-002 detected this and held dispatch because lead-bwu was still pending in the templates BC inbox.

Design question: should the protocol/template
- (a) explicitly enforce serial in-flight (one message at a time per BC; lead shop blocks on outbox before next send), formalizing the current presumption?
- (b) relax the singleton presumption (Implementer template handles N-message inbox; BC-router picks one to dispatch per turn)?
- (c) introduce a queue concept in shop-msg (lead can deposit multiple; BC processes in FIFO order; protocol exposes 'next' subcommand)?

(a) is the lowest-touch and matches current template behavior. (c) is the most flexible and may align with the brief-001 invariant (shop-msg encapsulates storage). (b) is a middle ground.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done(blocked) protocol gap) — same shape of latent protocol constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Currently lead-bwu remains pending in templates BC inbox; brief-002 dispatch is held until it lands. This bead captures the structural concern, not the operational hold.

title: shop-msg not on default PATH — requires venv activation for cold dispatches
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

shop-msg is installed at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg but the venv is not activated by default. Any agent dispatched cold (fresh subagent, new shell) must 'source .venv/bin/activate' first or invoke the absolute path; otherwise 'shop-msg ...' fails with command-not-found. The lead-architect's reconciliation pass hit this and worked around it.

What needs to be done: pick one of (a) ensure venv activation is automatic for any lead-shop session (devcontainer postCreate / shell rc), (b) install shop-msg outside the venv so it is on PATH unconditionally, (c) update subagent templates and CLAUDE.md to always invoke the absolute path or activate first. (c) is the lowest-touch / no install changes; (a) or (b) are more systemic. Choose during pickup.

title: Scenario register surface for templates BC (lead-architect activity currently degenerate)
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

The post-lead-kq0 lead-architect template names 'Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work' as a §3.2 activity. No register surface exists under features/templates/ — only the raw .gherkin files (01-16). The activity is structurally degenerate for the templates BC until a register is introduced; the role names a responsibility the mechanism doesn't yet realize.

What needs to be done: decide between (a) introduce a per-BC scenario register surface in the lead shop carrying authored / dispatched / signed-off status (designed once for all BCs, not just templates), or (b) accept reconciliation against the templates BC as degenerate by design and document why. If (a), the design likely interacts with the lead-shop drain workflow noted as deferred in findings/from-mechanism-observation-v1.md §6.
